concepts.title: Condition for line to be Tangent and Secant of ellipse
concepts.conceptBody:
Intersection of a line and an ellipse

The abscissae of points of the intersection of the line $y=mx+c$ and the ellipse $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$, are given by the quadratic equation $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{(mx+c)}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ Or $\left( {{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}} \right){{x}^{2}}+2{{a}^{2}}mcx+{{a}^{2}}\left( {{c}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}} \right)=$ $0\left( * \right)$. If the roots be ${{x}_{1}},{{x}_{2}}$ then

${{x}_{1}}+{{x}_{2}}=-\frac{2{{a}^{2}}mc}{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}};{{x}_{1}}{{x}_{2}}=\frac{{{a}^{2}}\left( {{c}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}} \right)}{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}$

If ordinates of the points be ${{\text{y}}_{1}}$ and ${{\text{y}}_{2}}$ then

${{\text{y}}_{1}}=\text{m}{{\text{x}}_{1}}+\text{c},{{\text{y}}_{2}}=\text{m}{{\text{x}}_{2}}+\text{c}\Rightarrow {{\text{y}}_{1}}+{{\text{y}}_{2}}=\text{m}\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}}-{{\text{x}}_{2}} \right)$

Now the distance between these points is equal to length intercepted by the line $\text{y}=\text{mx}+\text{c}$ on the ellipse $\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}+\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=1$ which is equal to

$\sqrt{{{\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}}-{{\text{x}}_{2}} \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( {{\text{y}}_{1}}-{{\text{y}}_{2}} \right)}^{2}}}=\sqrt{{{\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}}-{{\text{x}}_{2}} \right)}^{2}}+{{\text{m}}^{2}}{{\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}}-{{\text{x}}_{2}} \right)}^{2}}}=\left| {{\text{x}}_{1}}-{{\text{x}}_{2}} \right|\sqrt{1+{{\text{m}}^{2}}}$

$=\sqrt{{{\left( {{\text{x}}_{1}}-{{\text{x}}_{2}} \right)}^{2}}-4{{\text{x}}_{1}}{{\text{x}}_{2}}}\sqrt{1+{{\text{m}}^{2}}}$

$=\frac{2\text{ab}\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}~{{\text{m}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}-{{\text{c}}^{2}}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}~{{\text{m}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}}$

(on putting the values of ${{x}_{1}}+{{x}_{2}}$ and ${{x}_{1}}{{x}_{2}}$)

If the line happens to be tangent to the ellipse the length intercepted by it on the ellipse $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ must be zero

$\Rightarrow {{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}-{{c}^{2}}=0\Rightarrow {{c}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}\Rightarrow c=\pm \sqrt{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}$

In case ${{c}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}$ the equal root of the quadratic equation $\left( * \right)$ is$x=-\frac{{{a}^{2}}mc}{{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}}=-\frac{{{a}^{2}}m}{c}$ (If the equation $\text{A}{{\text{x}}^{2}}+\text{Bx}+\text{C}=0$ has equal root then it is equal to $-\text{B}/2\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}$ ). Also,

$y=mx+c=m\left( -\frac{{{a}^{2}}m}{c} \right)+c=\frac{-{{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{c}^{2}}}{c}=\frac{{{b}^{2}}}{c}$

Thus we have the following important result.

The line $y=mx+c$ will be a tangent to the ellipse $\frac{{{x}^{2}}}{{{a}^{2}}}+\frac{{{y}^{2}}}{{{b}^{2}}}=1$ if ${{c}^{2}}={{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}}$ and it will touch the ellipse at $\left( -\frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ m}}{\text{c}},\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{\text{c}} \right)$. The line $\text{y}=\text{mx}\pm \sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{m}}^{2}}+{{\text{b}}^{2}}}$ will be a tangent to the ellipse $\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}+\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=$ 1 for all values of $m$.

NOTE : If m = 0 then equation (3) gives

${{b}^{2}}{{x}^{2}}+{{c}^{2}}{{a}^{2}}-{{a}^{2}}{{b}^{2}}=0$

or ${{b}^{2}}{{x}^{2}}+\left( {{a}^{2}}{{m}^{2}}+{{b}^{2}} \right){{a}^{2}}-{{b}^{2}}=0$

∴ $\text{x}=\pm \frac{{{\text{a}}^{2}}~\text{m}}{~\text{b}}$

which gives two values of $\text{x}$.
